syllabus: This is being sent over the OpenAI API from a dart CLI app. Help me debug by writing unique responses, whatever you want to say!
transcript: [{role: user, text: Hello!, responseId: null, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:42:51.825034, meta: {}}, {role: assistant, text: Hello there! How's your day going?, responseId: resp_0a936c76e57d6a080069818adab5d4819098661ca7b3a09d54, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:42:51.825034, meta: {}}, {role: user, text: Pretty good! Technically, it's not day--it's midnight, but I just got a CLI working so I can talk to you, and later implement you into a wrapper!, responseId: null, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:43:26.491568, meta: {}}, {role: assistant, text: That sounds exciting! Midnight coding sessions can be the most productive. What kind of features are you planning to implement in your wrapper?, responseId: resp_0a936c76e57d6a080069818afc723c8190abf2f02c18573930, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:43:26.491568, meta: {}}, {role: user, text: It's going to be a Flutter mobile applictaion which allows students to use AI assistants as helpers while doing their homework in an environment monitored by their teachers. Not only can students use it to chat, but they can also grant teachers access to specific chats, meaning that if AI is detected in their work the teacher can make sure that A) it was using this application (StuddyBuddy) and B) they weren't somehow bypassing teh syllabus set by the teacher., responseId: null, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:45:10.976639, meta: {}}, {role: assistant, text: That’s a fantastic idea! It sounds like a great way to balance the benefits of AI assistance with academic integrity. The ability for teachers to monitor specific chats could really help ensure that students are using the tool responsibly. Have you thought about how you’ll handle privacy and data security for both students and teachers?, responseId: resp_0a936c76e57d6a080069818b65641481908bb26567641cfea4, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:45:10.976639, meta: {}}, {role: user, text: My plan was just to use Dart's built in HTTPS protocols on transit from client to server, and then when storing the data in our own db we'll use Supabase and their built in encryption system. Sound good enough?, responseId: null, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:46:20.722707, meta: {}}, {role: assistant, text: That sounds like a solid plan! Using HTTPS for secure data transmission is essential, and leveraging Supabase’s built-in encryption for stored data adds an extra layer of security. Just make sure to also consider user authentication and access controls to ensure that only authorized users can access sensitive information. Have you thought about how you'll implement user roles for students and teachers?, responseId: resp_0a936c76e57d6a080069818baac18081908509141f9a4a0ded, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:46:20.722707, meta: {}}, {role: user, text: It'll just be accounts with a set enum on creation. Depending on the account type, you'll have a different dashboard., responseId: null, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:47:09.353621, meta: {}}, {role: assistant, text: That’s a straightforward approach! Using enums for account types can help streamline the logic for different dashboards and functionalities. It might also be helpful to implement role-based access control (RBAC) to manage permissions effectively. Have you started designing the UI for the dashboards yet?, responseId: resp_0a936c76e57d6a080069818bdb4e78819094a48bb2bc394ed3, timestamp: 2026-02-03T00:47:09.353621, meta: {}}]
